title: UAGF-CGSA → AAA API Contract
description: JSON schema defining the output of S4 CGSA (Constraint-Based Governance Scoring Agent) consumed by S5 AAA (Autonomous AI Auditor) as the input to Phase 5 (Governance) of the 6-phase UAGF-TAM audit protocol. Agreed between S4 and S5 students at joint meeting by Week 4.

metadata.description: Identifies the organisation, system under audit, and the CGSA run that produced this report.

assessment_id.description: Unique identifier for this CGSA assessment run. Used by AAA to deduplicate and trace the governance phase input.

organisation_name.description: Name of the organisation whose governance documentation was assessed.

system_under_audit.description: Name or identifier of the specific AI system being audited. Used by AAA to match governance findings to the correct system in the 6-phase report.

cgsa_version.description: Semantic version of the CGSA tool that produced this output.

assessment_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the CGSA assessment completed.

risk_tier.description: EU AI Act risk tier of the system under audit. Determines which hard constraints are active in the CSP model. AAA uses this to confirm it matches the Phase 1 (Scope) classification.

document_sources.description: List of governance document filenames parsed by the CGSA LangChain pipeline to produce this assessment.

uagf_gmm_version.description: Version of the 38-control UAGF-GMM library used for scoring.

overall_scores.description: Top-level summary scores consumed by AAA for the Phase 5 executive summary and the audit report compliance matrix.

composite_maturity_score.description: Weighted average maturity score across all 38 controls. Scale: 0=Absent, 1=Initial, 2=Developing, 3=Defined, 4=Optimised.

composite_maturity_label.description: Human-readable label corresponding to the composite_maturity_score band.

eu_ai_act_coverage_pct.description: Percentage of EU AI Act Articles 9, 10, and 13 requirements that are satisfied by the assessed governance documentation, based on control scores meeting or exceeding their thresholds.

csp_satisfiable.description: Whether the python-constraint CSP solver found a valid, constraint-satisfying assignment for all 38 controls. FALSE means at least one hard constraint is violated — the system is non-compliant for its risk tier. AAA uses this as a binary gate for the Phase 5 verdict.

governance_verdict.description: Overall governance compliance verdict for this system. Derived from csp_satisfiable and eu_ai_act_coverage_pct. AAA inserts this directly into the Phase 5 findings section of the audit report.

controls_assessed.description: Total number of controls scored in this assessment (should equal 38 for a full run).

controls_meeting_threshold.description: Number of controls meeting or exceeding their required threshold for the given risk_tier.

controls_below_threshold.description: Number of controls failing to meet the minimum required threshold. AAA uses this count in the Phase 5 gap summary.

domains.description: The 6 governance domains of the UAGF-GMM, each containing their respective controls. AAA iterates these to populate the Phase 5 governance findings table.

domain_score.description: Mean maturity score across all controls within this domain.

domain_eu_ai_act_articles.description: EU AI Act articles primarily satisfied by controls in this domain.

controls.description: Individual UAGF-GMM controls belonging to this domain.

control_name.description: Short descriptive name of the control.

control_description.description: Full description of what this control assesses, including which governance behaviours are expected at each maturity level.

source_frameworks.description: The subset of the 12 synthesised governance standards from which this control was derived.

maturity_score.description: Ordinal maturity score assigned by the CSP solver. 0=Absent, 1=Initial, 2=Developing, 3=Defined, 4=Optimised.

maturity_descriptor.description: Full text description of what the assigned maturity level means for this specific control. Drawn from the UAGF-GMM 5-level descriptor table.

evidence_summary.description: Condensed excerpt or paraphrase of the evidence text extracted from governance documents by the LangChain parser that supports this maturity score. AAA may include this in the Phase 5 findings table.
evidence_summary.example: Policy doc Section 3.2 references data quality checks but no automated validation pipeline is documented.

evidence_source_document.description: Filename of the source document from which this evidence was extracted.

evidence_page_reference.description: Page number or section reference within the source document, if extractable.

confidence.description: LangChain parser confidence score for the evidence extraction. Values below 0.6 indicate low-confidence extraction; AAA should flag these as requiring human reviewer verification.

eu_ai_act_articles.description: EU AI Act articles that this control directly addresses. Used by AAA to construct the regulatory compliance matrix in the Phase 5 audit report section.

hard_constraint.description: CSP hard constraint definition for this control under the assessed risk_tier. Encodes the minimum maturity score required for regulatory compliance.

applicable.description: Whether a hard constraint applies to this control under the assessed risk_tier. High-risk systems have more hard constraints active than minimal/limited systems.

threshold_score.description: Minimum maturity_score required to satisfy this constraint. NULL if applicable=false.

satisfied.description: Whether maturity_score >= threshold_score. NULL if applicable=false. FALSE means a hard constraint violation — this control is a blocking compliance gap.

eu_ai_act_obligation.description: The specific EU AI Act obligation that this hard constraint encodes, as cited in the regulation text.
eu_ai_act_obligation.example: Article 10 point 2: Training, validation and testing data shall be subject to appropriate data governance and management practices.

gap_severity.description: Gap severity classification used by CGSA to rank the remediation roadmap. NULL if control meets or exceeds threshold. Critical = hard constraint violated on a high-risk system. High = hard constraint violated on a limited-risk system, or soft gap of ≥2 levels below optimised on a high-risk system. Medium = soft gap of 1 level on a high-risk system. Low = any gap on a minimal-risk system.

gap_detail.description: Human-readable explanation of the gap: what is missing, what is required, and the regulatory consequence. NULL if no gap. AAA uses this text directly in the Phase 5 remediation section.
gap_detail.example: No automated data validation pipeline is documented. Article 10 requires data governance practices for high-risk systems. Required maturity: 3 (Defined). Current maturity: 2 (Developing).

eu_ai_act_compliance_matrix.description: Consolidated view of EU AI Act compliance status by article. AAA inserts this matrix directly into the Phase 5 section of the audit report PDF. Only Articles 9, 10, and 13 are hard-constraint-encoded in CGSA v1.0; additional articles are informationally mapped.

article_10.description: Article 10 — Data and data governance obligations.

article_13.description: Article 13 — Transparency and provision of information to deployers.

article_14.description: Article 14 — Human oversight obligations. Informationally mapped; not hard-constraint-encoded in CGSA v1.0.

article_17.description: Article 17 — Quality management system obligations. Informationally mapped.

hard_constraint_results.description: Summary of CSP solver results. AAA uses this as the primary input for the binary compliance gate in Phase 5.

csp_satisfiable.description: Mirrors overall_scores.csp_satisfiable. TRUE = all hard constraints satisfied = system is governance-compliant for its risk tier.

total_hard_constraints.description: Total number of hard constraints active for the assessed risk_tier.

violated_constraints.description: List of hard constraint violations. AAA iterates this list to populate the Phase 5 critical findings table. Each item pinpoints exactly which control, which article, and what the gap is.

score_delta.description: required_score minus actual_score. Positive = gap. AAA uses this to sort violations by severity.

violation_description.example: Data governance practices are developing but not formally defined. A documented data validation pipeline is required for high-risk system compliance.

satisfied_constraints.description: List of hard constraints that ARE satisfied. Included for completeness and for the positive findings section of the audit report.

remediation_roadmap.description: Ordered list of remediation actions, ranked by gap_severity (critical first) and then by score_delta. AAA extracts this list into the Phase 5 remediation section of the audit report. Items with gap_severity=critical are surfaced as Phase 5 blocking findings.

action.description: Specific, actionable remediation step the organisation must take to close this gap.
action.example: Define and document a formal data validation pipeline including completeness checks, bias scanning, and version control for training datasets. Evidence should include a written Data Management Plan referenced in the AI system technical documentation.

eu_ai_act_article.description: The specific EU AI Act article obligation that this remediation action addresses.

effort_estimate.description: Relative effort estimate for implementing the remediation action. Low = documentation update only. Medium = process change required. High = tooling or system change required.

timeline_weeks.description: Suggested implementation timeline in weeks. NULL if not estimable.

priority_rationale.description: Explanation of why this item is ranked at its position. AAA may include this in the Phase 5 narrative.
priority_rationale.example: Hard constraint violation on a high-risk system. Non-compliance with Article 10 data governance obligations is a blocking finding that must be resolved before conformity assessment can be passed.

aaa_phase5_handoff.description: Dedicated handoff block specifically designed for AAA Phase 5 (Governance) consumption. Contains pre-computed summary fields that AAA can insert directly into the audit report without re-processing the full domain/control arrays. This is the primary integration surface between S4 and S5.

phase5_verdict.description: Binary-ternary verdict for AAA Phase 5 (Governance). PASS = csp_satisfiable=true and eu_ai_act_coverage_pct >= 80. PASS_WITH_OBSERVATIONS = csp_satisfiable=true but coverage < 80, or soft gaps only. FAIL = csp_satisfiable=false (one or more hard constraints violated). AAA uses this to set the traffic-light status in the audit report executive summary.

phase5_narrative_summary.description: Pre-written 3–5 sentence narrative summary of the governance findings, ready for AAA to insert into the Phase 5 section of the audit report with minimal editing. Written in audit report register.
phase5_narrative_summary.example: The governance documentation of the system under audit demonstrates developing maturity (composite score 2.7/4.0) across the 38 UAGF-GMM controls. The CGSA CSP solver identified 3 hard constraint violations, indicating non-compliance with EU AI Act Articles 9 and 10 requirements for high-risk systems. EU AI Act regulatory coverage stands at 68.4%, below the 80% threshold required for a compliant verdict. The most critical gap is in Data Quality Management (C07), where no formal data validation pipeline is documented. Remediation of the 3 critical findings is required before the system can be assessed as governance-compliant.

blocking_findings_count.description: Count of critical/hard-constraint-violating findings. AAA displays this prominently in the Phase 5 header.

blocking_findings.description: Condensed list of critical findings for AAA Phase 5 report table. Each item maps to a violated hard constraint.

finding.description: One-sentence finding statement in audit register.
finding.example: No formal data validation pipeline is documented; data governance practices are developing but not defined.

remediation_action.description: Condensed remediation action for the audit report table.
remediation_action.example: Define and document a formal data validation pipeline including completeness checks, bias scanning, and dataset version control.

positive_findings.description: Controls that meet or exceed threshold — for the positive findings section of the Phase 5 audit report.

finding.description: One-sentence positive finding statement in audit register.

low_confidence_controls.description: Controls where LangChain extraction confidence < 0.60. AAA must flag these in the Phase 5 limitations section and recommend human reviewer verification for each.

flag_reason.description: Reason for low confidence — e.g., sparse documentation, ambiguous language, missing section.
flag_reason.example: Governance document contains no dedicated section on risk management; score inferred from scattered references in meeting minutes.

aaa_recommended_follow_up.description: CGSA-recommended follow-up actions for AAA Phase 5 — e.g., requesting additional governance documents, scheduling interviews, or escalating to human reviewer. AAA may incorporate these into the Phase 5 recommendations section.

cgsa_report_url.description: URL of the full CGSA Streamlit PDF report for this assessment, if deployed. AAA includes this as a hyperlink in the Phase 5 section ('Full governance scoring report available at...').

status.description: Compliance status for this article based on the control scores and hard constraint results.

controls_mapped.description: Control IDs mapped to this article.

controls_satisfied.description: Subset of controls_mapped that meet their threshold.

coverage_pct.description: Percentage of mapped controls that satisfy their threshold for this article.

hard_constraints_violated.description: Control IDs with hard constraint violations under this article.

article_summary.description: One-sentence compliance summary for this article. AAA inserts this into the regulatory compliance matrix table.
article_summary.example: Article 9 is partially satisfied: risk identification processes are documented (C01, C03) but risk mitigation and residual risk assessment (C02, C04) are below the required threshold.

example_payload._comment: Minimal valid example payload illustrating the schema structure. Full example with all 38 controls is in uagf_cgsa_aaa_schema_example.json

aaa_phase5_handoff.phase5_narrative_summary: The governance documentation demonstrates developing maturity (2.7/4.0). Three hard constraint violations were identified under Articles 9 and 10. EU AI Act coverage is 68.4%, below the 80% compliance threshold. Remediation of three critical findings is required before the system can receive a compliant governance verdict.

blocking_findings.finding: No formal data validation pipeline is documented; practices are developing but not defined.
blocking_findings.remediation_action: Define and document a formal data validation pipeline with completeness checks, bias scanning, and dataset version control.

positive_findings.finding: Risk identification processes are formally documented and consistently applied across the AI system lifecycle.

low_confidence_controls.flag_reason: Governance document contains no dedicated incident response section; score inferred from scattered references in internal audit notes.

aaa_recommended_follow_up.recommendation: Request the organisation's Data Management Plan and dataset documentation before finalising Phase 5 findings.
aaa_recommended_follow_up.rationale: Three data governance controls (C06, C07, C08) scored below threshold with moderate confidence. Additional documentation may change the verdict on C06 and C08.
